run_metadata.coverage_summary: Daily incremental discovery run. Tier 1 federal + all state/territory portals surveyed via web search (AusTender, buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders / qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au / supply.qld.gov.au, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, QTOL NT, Tenders ACT). Tier 2 sampled: local government via VendorPanel public tenders, GBE water (Sydney Water, Melbourne Water, SA Water), CSIRO, Australia Post, NBN Co, Transgrid, universities/TAFE, NSW Health / Queensland Health, Transport for NSW / Sydney Trains, ICN Gateway. Tier 3 (mandatory) attempted: SAP Ariba / SAP Business Network Discovery, EstimateOne (construction head-contractor packages) - no publicly-visible individual corporate RFx could be captured (all buyer detail is registration- or invitation-gated), so corporate channels remain recorded in discovered_sources from prior runs, not invented as tenders. COVERAGE LIMITATION (unchanged from recent runs): this run executed under a restrictive network egress policy - direct HTTPS access to primary tender portals was blocked. Every portal detail host (www.tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw.gov.au, tenders.vic.gov.au, qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au, tenders.wa.gov.au, tenders.sa.gov.au, tenders.tas.gov.au, tendersonline.nt.gov.au, tenders.act.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au) returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (origin bot-protection), so no primary notice or tender document could be opened directly. Only WebSearch (a separate backend) was usable. Every opportunity below was surfaced from indexed web-search snippets carrying real detail-page URLs and consistent titles; headline identity fields are corroborated across snippets, but closing times, values, deliverables, eligibility, evaluation criteria and contacts were NOT verifiable against the primary notice and are flagged pending a detail pass. Emphasis this run was on opportunities NOT already in the aggregate: three new corroborated items were captured (Army Innovation Day 2026 RFI / LCD-26-0030; Werribee Open Range Zoo emergency access works, Buying for Victoria; Welding Aptitude Testing Program / RFT/WLD/2026, Australian Submarine Agency). Several other surfaced items (Blacktown Christmas decorations RFT, TfNSW Bradfield Bus Interchange EOI, HealthShare natural gas, Sydney Water CEZ / drinking-water EPCM EOIs, SA Water Millicent WWTP, Harbour Trust SHFT26001, QTOL Power and Water) were already captured on earlier runs and were excluded as duplicates rather than re-listed.
gaps: Direct HTTPS fetch of all primary tender portals blocked (HTTP 403 origin bot-protection) via WebFetch; all detail extraction was headline-only from WebSearch snippets - no primary notice, document, closing time, value, deliverable or contact could be verified against the source. Every captured tender is flagged pending a detail pass.

AusTender: several current ATMs surfaced only by reference without a corroborated title AND future closing date in the snippet (ATM_2026_6026; MSD/RFT/55619/1; 57097; RFT_2026_0044); not recorded to avoid guessing. RFT-2026-0080 (Federal Court - Primary Dispute Resolution / Psychologists services) and RFT-2026-0031 surfaced but are already CLOSED (closed Apr 2026), so excluded.

Queensland (qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au): two live detail URLs surfaced (VP430497 id=53437; DOC62256541 id=53424) but neither title nor closing date was present in the snippet and the detail pages are bot-protected; deferred to next run.

Tenders WA / Tenders SA / Tenders Tasmania / QTOL NT: portal landing pages reachable via search but no individually-named open tender with a corroborated title AND future closing date surfaced in snippets this run (Tasmania reported '1 new tender in last 7 days' but the title was not exposed); QTOL Power and Water (closing 2026-07-23) remains open but was already captured on a prior run; deferred.

ICN Gateway: current EOIs surfaced (e.g. Cairns Airport Terminal 1 Refresh Programme; Western Sydney International Airport work packages) but no corroborated closing date or package detail was visible and the project pages are login-gated for full scope; recorded as discovery signal only, not fabricated as tenders. ICN Gateway itself is already a known source.

SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery: no Australia-specific open posting could be read without an authenticated supplier session; the public board is registration-gated and is already recorded in discovered_sources from earlier runs.

Tier 3 corporate buyers (BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside, Santos, Woolworths, Coles, Qantas, CBA/Westpac/NAB/ANZ, Telstra/Optus, NBN Co, Australia Post, Transgrid): individual opportunities are registration- or invitation-gated; portals already recorded in discovered_sources; no corporate tenders fabricated. Management-consultancy RFPs remain invitation-only and out of scope.
EstimateOne / iTWOcx construction subcontractor packages: platform is supplier-account-gated; no publicly-readable open package with corroborated detail surfaced; recorded as source only.

Aggregator/dashboard sites (australiantenders.com.au, tendersontime.com, tendersinfo.com, govmarket.com.au, bidio.ai, tenders.net, qld2032.com, tenderhub.com.au, illion.tenderlink.com) appeared in results and were used ONLY as discovery signals to confirm buyer-originated sources; none are recorded as sources of truth.
recommended_seed_additions: East Gippsland Shire Council - Procurement (VendorPanel) - VIC local-government buyer migrating from TenderLink to VendorPanel; from March 2026 it advertises most quotations/tenders on VendorPanel. Not previously in the known council seed list.

Federation University Australia - Tenders (VendorPanel Marketplace Public Tenders) - VIC university buyer publishing its tenders via a free VendorPanel public marketplace; not previously among the known university seeds.

tenders.title: Army Innovation Day 2026 (AID26) - RFI for Autonomous Collaborative Systems for Contested Littoral Warfare
tenders.buyer: Department of Defence (Australian Army - Land Capability Division)
tenders.description: Request for information under Army Innovation Day 2026 (theme: 'Autonomous Mass in Littoral Warfare') seeking innovative technologies at Technology Readiness Level 4-6 for autonomous systems that operate collaboratively in contested littoral environments, offering scalable, affordable mass for small units without exposing soldiers to direct fire.
tenders.briefing: Selected respondents may be invited to the AID26 exhibition space at Land Forces 2026 (6-8 October, Perth Convention and Exhibition Centre) and to Army-led field experimentation/demonstration activities.
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the AusTender current ATM list; buyer-originated Commonwealth (Australian Army) notice on tenders.gov.au with a live ATM deep link (LCD-26-0030). Title, theme and TRL band corroborated across the AusTender snippet and the Australian Army Research Centre AID page.
tenders.notes: RFI / market-sounding, not a binding solicitation. Headline-only: AusTender detail page returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (origin bot-protection). Per search snippet, responses open in July 2026 with invitations to participate issued from 17 August 2026; the exact AusTender closing date/time could not be verified against the notice, so closing_date/publish_date left null. Confirm all detail on the AusTender ATM page.

tenders.title: Emergency Access and Associated Works - Werribee Open Range Zoo
tenders.buyer: Zoos Victoria (Werribee Open Range Zoo) - buyer inferred; procured via Department of Transport and Planning pre-qualified construction contractor register
tenders.description: Request for tender for emergency access and associated works at Werribee Open Range Zoo, run through the Victorian Department of Transport and Planning pre-qualified contractor arrangement and advertised on Buying for Victoria.
tenders.eligibility: Open to pre-qualified Department of Transport and Planning construction contractors (per search snippet - confirm on notice)
tenders.submission_method: E-lodgement via Buying for Victoria portal
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of Buying for Victoria open tenders with a live tender/view deep link (id=324739); buyer-originated Victorian Government works notice. Title, pre-qualified-contractor eligibility and 31/07/2026 closing corroborated in the search snippet.
tenders.notes: Headline-only: Buying for Victoria detail page returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch. Buyer inferred from the site name (Werribee Open Range Zoo = Zoos Victoria); scope, value, insurance/licence conditions and contacts were not verifiable against the notice. Confirm all detail on the Buying for Victoria notice.

tenders.title: Design and Delivery of a Welding Aptitude Testing Program
tenders.category: Training and assessment services (welding aptitude testing)
tenders.description: Request for tender by the Australian Submarine Agency to engage a suitably qualified supplier to design and deliver a Welding Aptitude Testing Program.
tenders.reasoning: Surfaced via WebSearch of the AusTender current ATM list; buyer-originated Commonwealth (Australian Submarine Agency) notice with a live ATM deep link (RFT/WLD/2026). Title, agency, publish date (2-Jul-2026) and closing (3-Aug-2026 4:00 PM ACT Local Time) corroborated in the search snippet.
tenders.notes: Headline-only: AusTender detail page returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch, so publish/closing dates come from a search snippet and are not verified against the primary notice; scope, deliverables, eligibility, evaluation criteria, value and contacts were not captured. Confirm all detail on the AusTender ATM page.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for newly-launched/migrated Australian council procurement portals surfaced the East Gippsland Shire Council media release 'Step into Council opportunities with VendorPanel' - the council is moving from TenderLink to VendorPanel via a staged rollout from December 2025, and from March 2026 advertises most quotations/tenders for goods, services and works on VendorPanel.
discovered_sources.notes: East Gippsland (Victoria) local-government buyer not previously in the known council seed list. Suppliers register free on VendorPanel. Confirm the exact council tenders/VendorPanel URL on the council website; a legacy TenderLink e-tendering site may remain live during transition.

discovered_sources.source_name: Federation University Australia - Tenders (VendorPanel Marketplace Public Tenders)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch of Australian university procurement pages surfaced Federation University's Strategic Procurement 'Tenders' page, which uses the VendorPanel Marketplace Public Tenders portal for its eTendering; supplier registration on the public marketplace is free.
discovered_sources.notes: Regional Victorian university buyer not previously among the known university seeds (JCU, USyd, Macquarie, ACU, UTS, RMIT, La Trobe, Monash). Publishes its tenders via a buyer-originated VendorPanel public marketplace; register to see full opportunity detail.
